text: Abdominal lymph node dissection plays a pivotal role in esophageal cancer surgery, as it directly impacts cancer recurrence and overall survival.
Among the various challenges encountered during this procedure, one particularly demanding aspect is the dissection around the porta hepatis.
This region is deeply situated, and achieving a thorough dissection poses technical difficulties.
However, the utilization of robotic devices can greatly enhance exposure in this area, enabling deep dissection down to the level of the proper hepatic artery.
With this technique, complete removal around the common hepatic artery becomes feasible.
Furthermore, the dissection can be extended to encompass the celiac axis.
This approach allows for a comprehensive visualization of the proximal part of the diaphragmatic cruise,
Although this type of dissection can also be performed using laparoscopy or open techniques, the robotic approach proves more accessible, even for beginners in laparoscopic surgery.
